text: Inline embedding into the Node backend for the default local path
description: Replace the separate Python embedder in the default local runtime with a Node-based embedding path in the backend, preserving current vector/search behavior well enough for normal local use.

reflections.context: Replace the separate Python embedder in the default local runtime with an in-process backend embedding path without removing semantic-search features.
reflections.outcome: Implemented a deterministic local embedding generator in the backend, preserved vector storage/search semantics, taught buildSearchIndex to regenerate outdated vectors, removed the Python embedder from default dev architecture, and updated release docs accordingly.
reflections.effectiveness: This worked well for the release goal because it removed the extra Python runtime immediately and was fully verifiable in the sandbox with backend build/tests. The main compromise is search quality: the new default is lighter and more reliable, but weaker than a model-backed embedder.
reflections.lesson: When replacing a runtime dependency, account for stored-artifact compatibility explicitly. Preserving old numeric vectors while distinguishing current-format embeddings avoided breaking existing data and tests.
reflections.pattern: This matches earlier release work: simplify the default local path first, then capture higher-fidelity options as explicit follow-up aims instead of blocking release on ideal architecture.

status.comment: Done. Backend now generates embeddings in-process, buildSearchIndex regenerates outdated vectors, docs/scripts no longer treat the Python embedder as part of the default architecture.
